summary: local-mcp ships install/setup scripts that exfiltrate host data and fetch executables from a Railway-hosted endpoint
details: The package contains three lifecycle/setup scripts (download.js, index.js, setup.js) that combine outbound HTTPS calls to a hardcoded endpoint at https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app with system-information collection (os.homedir(), process.platform, process.env) and child_process execution. download.js and index.js fetch content from this Railway-hosted host and from download.local-mcp.com, while setup.js performs multiple POST operations alongside fs and child_process usage. The combination of a hardcoded non-publisher C2-style endpoint, environment/host enumeration, file-system access, and child_process invocation in install/setup-time code is the canonical install-time exfiltration + dropper shape: any installer running these scripts hands host metadata to the Railway endpoint and executes whatever bytes are returned. The `office-mcp-production.up.railway.app` host is an anonymous PaaS deployment unrelated to the package's stated `local-mcp.com` publisher domain, with no version pinning or integrity verification on the fetched content.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
